meta.description: Maps ESS categories to expected resource counts for ESI level determination (ESI-3/4/5). Resources include: labs, imaging, IV fluids/meds, procedures, specialist consults. The min_resources field determines ESI level: >=2 -> ESI-3, 1 -> ESI-4, 0 -> ESI-5.

detail: labs: troponin (serial x2), BNP, D-dimer, CBC, BMP
imaging: CXR, ECG (counted as procedure by some; imaging here)
iv: IV access + fluids, possible heparin drip
labs: CBC, CRP, blood culture, urinalysis; LP if meningism suspected
iv: IV fluids if dehydrated, antipyretics
labs: coagulation panel (especially if anticoagulated), CBC
imaging: X-ray of affected area, CT head if head strike or anticoagulated
labs: CBC, lipase, LFTs, lactate, CRP, urinalysis
imaging: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast
iv: IV fluids, analgesia, possible antiemetics
labs: ABG/VBG, D-dimer, CBC, CRP, procalcitonin
imaging: CXR, CTPA if PE suspected
iv: nebulized bronchodilators, supplemental O2, IV antibiotics if pneumonia
labs: glucose, coagulation, CBC, electrolytes
imaging: CT head without contrast (stat), CTA head/neck if stroke suspected
iv: IV access, possible thrombolytics within window
specialist: neurology consult (stroke team activation if applicable)
labs: urinalysis, urine culture, CBC, CRP if systemic signs
iv: IV fluids and IV antibiotics if pyelonephritis/urosepsis
procedures: wound irrigation, sutures or staples, tetanus if indicated
imaging: X-ray of affected joint/bone
labs: toxicology screen, glucose, thyroid if first presentation
specialist: psychiatric consult
procedures: otoscopy, anterior rhinoscopy, or foreign body removal
specialist: ophthalmology consult or slit-lamp examination
labs: basic panel
imaging: as indicated by presentation
